meta.title: Sample Claude Code Setup Audit
meta.lede: This sample uses fictional data so you can preview the dashboard renderer safely.

checkup.comment: The sample setup is usable, but the context tax and stale permissions would deserve a focused cleanup.

decisions.why: The auditor needs explicit exclusions before scanning.

options.body: Keep the default personal, key, and patient-data exclusions.
Add project-specific private directories before running.

top.title: Always-on instructions are doing too much work
top.body: Several long notes are loaded every session even though they are only needed for rare workflows.

A.title: high impact x low effort
items: Move stale examples out of always-on context

B.title: high impact x mid effort
items: Split broad skills into tighter triggers

C.title: high impact x high effort
items: Design a repeatable quarterly checkup cadence

actions.title: Quarantine stale always-on notes
steps: Move dated examples to an archive
actions.prompt: Back up the current CLAUDE.md, move stale examples to a dated quarantine folder with a MANIFEST.tsv, verify the file still renders, then report the token-count delta. Do not delete anything.

phases.name: Phase 0
steps: Back up settings and command files

phases.name: Phase 1
steps: Handle reversible context and permission items only

domains.name: CLAUDE.md hierarchy
domains.summary: The sample context contains useful guidance mixed with rare workflow details.

findings.title: Rare workflow notes load every session
findings.detail: Three long examples are loaded before every task.
findings.recommendation: Move examples behind a skill or command-specific reference.

domains.name: Settings & permissions
domains.sub: Allowed tools, denied paths, and guardrails
domains.summary: The sample permissions are mostly scoped but contain stale entries.

findings.title: Dead allow-list entries
findings.detail: Several allowed paths no longer exist.
findings.recommendation: Back up settings, remove dead entries, then validate JSON.

domains.name: Skills
domains.summary: The sample skill set is healthy enough, with one trigger collision.

findings.title: Two skills share the same trigger word
findings.detail: Both skills mention the same broad trigger phrase.
findings.recommendation: Make one description narrower and re-run the trigger scan.
